expectedBadBehavior: Apologize, abandon the protocol, or restart after feedback about the questioning.
expectedGoodBehavior: Name the current phase in no more than two plain sentences, then resume exactly there.

turns.text: Scout report: the score path is known, but the submission-safe tradeoff needs one owner decision. What I found: the verified best safe score trails the best local score, and the local candidate has a cap warning.

blindspots.text: The cap warning could make the local best unusable.

questions.text: EXPERIMENT_LOG.md:34 flags a cap warning; should I keep only submission-safe candidates?

turns.text: This is confusing; why are you asking?
We are in probe because the scorer history leaves one plan-changing choice. I will keep it to that one evidence-backed decision and continue from there.
